danger_signals.visual_form: Ground/mountain surfaces approaching rapidly via Mode 7 scaling effect
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent crash hazard; player must correct altitude or trajectory

danger_signals.visual_form: Aircraft/glider pitch and roll distortion in viewport scaling
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of control; requires corrective input to avoid stall or spin

danger_signals.visual_form: HUD indicator reduction (qualitative visual feedback during flight segments)
danger_signals.meaning: Limited time remaining; must reach landing zone or designated checkpoint

danger_signals.visual_form: Edge of scrolling playfield; map boundaries indicated by terrain drop-off or ocean
danger_signals.meaning: Leaving mission zone results in failure condition

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated ground area with contrasting terrain color or flat surface within environment
safety_signals.meaning: Target landing location; successful touchdown here completes objective

safety_signals.visual_form: On-screen positional indicator or environmental landmark highlighting objective direction
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation cue toward next goal or checkpoint

safety_signals.visual_form: Reduced scaling of ground features via Mode 7 (visual indication of vertical distance)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe flight altitude; away from collision hazards

safety_signals.visual_form: Smooth scaling/rotation transitions without erratic viewport distortion
safety_signals.meaning: Aircraft responds predictably to input; normal flight conditions maintained

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Civilian aircraft (light planes, hang gliders, jet packs, rocket belt); mission briefing locations representing training academy facilities

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Attack helicopter model; distinct from civilian aircraft
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Helicopter sprite appears in bonus stages; represents optional advanced challenge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overhead isometric view via Mode 7; landscape rotating/scaling to show altitude changes and directional movement; aircraft sprite centered in viewport
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player has continuous control over throttle, pitch, and roll; fuel consumption decreases over time; hazard terrain remains static but approaches as altitude drops

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Similar Mode 7 perspective; terrain appears farther due to altitude; glider sprite smaller/more vulnerable visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No engine power; player depends on thermal updrafts and momentum; drift and descent are primary concerns; control is gentler but more momentum-dependent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Free-fall perspective; landscape approaches rapidly; sky/cloud backdrop indicates altitude; parachute deployment changes viewport scale dramatically
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Initial free-fall with lateral movement only; parachute deployment slows descent; landing precision required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Low-altitude flight; Mode 7 scaling shows immediate proximity to ground; closer visual perspective emphasizes precision requirements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Precise hover control required; rapid fuel burn; small margin for error in terrain avoidance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Abrupt zoom/scale freeze as aircraft impacts terrain; transition to failure state screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Input becomes unresponsive; mission restarts or returns to menu

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aircraft comes to rest on designated landing zone; viewport stabilizes; screen transitions to results/scoring display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Input locked; scoring/grade assignment displayed; progression to next mission unlocked

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Bonus stage active - attack helicopter combat
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Helicopter sprite enemy visible in playfield; distinct from player aircraft; may feature different coloring or design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Helicopter exhibits autonomous movement patterns; represents scoring target or evasion challenge; player must navigate or intercept

environmental_storytelling.example: Mountain terrain, valleys, and flat plains are rendered through scaling/rotation to convey geography; player learns layout through repeated flights and visual exploration
Sequential unlock of aircraft types (light plane → hang glider → skydiving → rocket belt) communicates escalating skill challenges; bonus helicopter stage rewards mastery
Distinctive terrain features (peaks, bodies of water, runway areas) serve as visual reference points; player builds mental map of mission space through visual familiarity
Each aircraft type has unique physics/controls; visual feedback (responsiveness, speed, drift patterns) communicates to player how each vehicle handles differently

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responds immediately to pitch/roll input; altitude maintained via throttle; gradual fuel depletion over time
Engine performance degrades; descent accelerates; control becomes sluggish (qualitative feedback)
Constant descent unless in thermal updraft; lateral drift due to wind; player must seek rising air currents to maintain altitude
Ascent rate increases; altitude gain visible through inverse Mode 7 scaling; player must position glider within updraft zone
Rapid descent; lateral drift only; rapid ground approach visible; parachute not yet deployed
Descent slows dramatically; viewport scale change reflects reduced velocity; fine control allows precision landing
Precise altitude maintenance required; fuel burns rapidly; immediate ground proximity visible; high crash risk
Moves autonomously in playfield; may evade or intercept player; represents obstacle or target depending on mission

notes: Pilotwings (SNES) is a flight simulation without explicit faction conflict or political scenarios. Danger and safety signals are purely environmental/mechanical. The game's world state is communicated almost entirely through visual perspective manipulation (Mode 7 scaling/rotation) and flight physics feedback. Visual communication is abstract and mechanical rather than narrative-driven. No hostile non-player factions; bonus helicopter is gameplay challenge, not faction antagonist. The paradigm listed as 'racing' appears inaccurate; this is an amateur flight simulator with mission-based progression.
